title: 'Latkana, bhatkana, atkana': PM Modi targets Congress over opposition to women quota bill
content: Prime Minister Modi expressed deep regret over the women's reservation bill's defeat, apologizing to the nation's women. He accused opposition parties like Congress, DMK, TMC, and SP of prioritizing self-interest over national progress and women's empowerment, highlighting their alleged celebration of the bill's failure as an insult to women's dignity

title: Strait talk turns straitjacket: Diplomacy stuck, ships struck, in Hormuz
content: The US-Iran standoff in the Strait of Hormuz remains unresolved just days before the ceasefire deadline, with tensions rising again after Iran rejected claims by US President Donald Trump that it had agreed to major concessions. Tehran announced it would tighten control over the waterway, citing US violations and “bogus victory claims.” The situation escalated after reports of gunfire forced Indian vessels to turn back, prompting a strong protest from New Delhi. Both sides are now on high alert, with uncertainty over whether the ceasefire will hold or lead to renewed conflict.

title: SRH bowlers hold nerve, choke CSK in final overs to seal 10-run thriller
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad secured a tense 10-run victory over Chennai Super Kings, with Eshan Malinga and Shivang Kumar's exceptional death-overs bowling proving decisive. Despite a strong chase from CSK, SRH's bowlers consistently took wickets, restricting them to 184 for 8. Earlier, Abhishek Sharma's explosive 59 and Heinrich Klaasen's 59 guided SRH to 194 for 9.

title: OpenAI exec Srinivas Narayanan leaves to spend time with ageing parents in India
content: Srinivas Narayanan, OpenAI's CTO for B2B applications, is departing the company next week after nearly three years. He plans to visit his parents in India before considering future professional endeavors. Narayanan, who previously served as VP of Engineering, shared his departure on LinkedIn, calling his tenure at the AI firm 'three incredible years'.

title: 'I was looking at a score around 220-230': Gaikwad after SRH loss
content: Chennai Super Kings lost by 10 runs to Sunrisers Hyderabad after a middle-overs slowdown derailed their chase. Ruturaj Gaikwad said restricting SRH to 194 felt good but scoring just four runs after 10 overs proved costly. Despite strong starts, CSK lost momentum. He praised the bowlers, especially Anshul Kamboj, for consistent performances and clarity in execution.

title: Iran envoy says US backtracked on agreed 10-point plan, stalling peace talks
content: Iran's Supreme Leader representative in India, Abdul Majeed Hakeem Ilahi, stated that recent Iran-US peace talks failed after Washington allegedly deviated from an agreed-upon 10-point plan. Ilahi claimed the US initially accepted the proposal but shifted its demands during negotiations in Pakistan, leading to the breakdown.

title: Microsoft’s & Apple’s coding tools will be ‘dead soon,': Creator of Antrophic's AI coding tool
content: Boris Cherny, creator of Claude Code, predicts traditional coding tools like VS Code and Xcode will soon be obsolete. He believes AI agents will soon handle entire development workflows, rendering current IDEs unnecessary. This shift will redefine software engineers into 'builders' who orchestrate AI, requiring adaptability and early experimentation from professionals.

title: IPL Points Table: SRH surge into top four, CSK remain seventh
content: Punjab Kings lead IPL 2026 with 9 points, while RCB and Rajasthan Royals trail closely. Sunrisers Hyderabad climbed to fourth after beating Chennai Super Kings by 10 runs. Abhishek Sharma and Heinrich Klaasen powered SRH to 194/9, while Eshan Malinga starred with the ball. CSK’s chase faltered despite a strong start, leaving them seventh in the standings.

title: Happy Akshaya Tritiya 2026: Top 50 wishes to share with your loved ones
content: Akshaya Tritiya, a holy Hindu festival celebrated on April 19th, signifies eternal prosperity and happiness. This day encourages good deeds, investments, and new beginnings, believed to bring lasting blessings. The article offers 50 wishes, messages, and quotes to share, emphasizing joy, abundance, and personal growth.

title: This US state is first to pause data centre construction: Why this legislation is big
content: Maine has become the first US state to temporarily halt new, large data centers, pausing expansion for 18 months. This move addresses concerns over the AI industry's environmental impact and rising utility costs. While other states debate similar measures, Maine's decision could spark federal political tension, as the Trump administration opposes state-level tech regulations.

title: Abhishek Sharma scripts history for SRH, becomes fastest ever to...
content: Abhishek Sharma smashed the fastest fifty in Sunrisers Hyderabad history, reaching the mark in just 15 balls against Chennai Super Kings in IPL 2026. He scored 59 off 22 balls, setting the tone early. In fine form this season, Abhishek continues to provide explosive starts and has already crossed 100 sixes for the franchise.

title: Dianna Russini–Mike Vrabel drama: Husband dragged into affair row
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and the former New York Times’ reporter and insider, Dianna Russini, continue to be a topic of discussion as their alleged affair rumors swirl now. While Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini have denied affair rumors, things have gotten worse as the popular NFL insider stepped down from her job at The Athletic. Amid this, there has been a new allegation against them.

title: Josh Allen, Hailee Steinfeld glow after welcoming first child
content: NFL star Josh Allen and actress Hailee Steinfeld welcomed their baby daughter, sharing the joyous news on Thursday. Just a day later, the new parents made a dazzling appearance at Josh's best friend Sam Darnold's wedding. The couple, radiating happiness, celebrated with fellow NFL players, even sharing baby photos, proving they can balance new parenthood with social commitments.

title: Floyd Mayweather Jr: $100K lawsuit after $7M tax debt before Pacquiao bout
content: Boxing legend Floyd Mayweather Jr is once again in legal trouble after a company accused him of not paying for private jet services. A complaint filed in Los Angeles County claims that Jet Set Aircraft Inc. is owed more than $105,000. The case is linked to a private charter flight in 2025. The lawsuit also names Mayweather’s company, TBE Aviation, and other individuals who were allegedly involved in arranging the trip.

title: Post-Dhurandhar fame: Gaurav Gera says love shaped his views
content: Gaurav Gera, currently receiving praise for his role as Aalam in Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar and its sequel Dhurandhar: The Revenge, is finally enjoying a phase of recognition that has been a long time coming. Amid the appreciation, the actor also opened up about a deeply personal chapter from his past and how it continues to shape his emotional boundaries today—especially when it comes to marriage.

title: Explained: On way to 4th largest, how India slipped to 6th rank & what it means
content: In fact, IMF’s latest World Economic Outlook (April 2026) sees India sitting at the sixth spot this financial year too. What has led to the sudden fall? Why has India dropped to the sixth position, falling behind the UK, instead of overtaking Japan to become the fourth largest economy?

title: INS Dhruv: India’s missile tracking ship, ensuring the country’s strategic might
content: India's INS Dhruv plays a crucial role in tracking long-range missile tests over the Bay of Bengal and Indian Ocean. This advanced vessel gathers vital telemetry data, aids in anti-ballistic missile system development, and provides early warning against hypersonic threats. Its capabilities also extend to monitoring Low Earth Orbit satellites, offering a significant strategic advantage to the nation's armed forces.

title: Young, overweight, at risk: India's silent health emergency
content: A new health report reveals over half of Indians under 30 are now overweight or obese, a concerning trend accelerating with sedentary lifestyles and poor diets. This early weight gain significantly increases the risk of metabolic diseases like fatty liver and diabetes at younger ages, posing a long-term public health challenge for the nation.

title: David Miller redeems himself with last-over finish as DC chase down 176 vs RCB
content: After losing three wickets in the powerplay, DC recovered through Rahul (57 off 34 balls), Stubbs (60 not out off 47 balls) and Axar Patel (26 retired hurt). Rahul steadied the innings after an early collapse and targeted Josh Hazlewood, hitting him for two sixes and three fours. He reached his fifty in 30 balls before falling to Krunal Pandya, caught by Virat Kohli at long-on.

title: Samay Raina recalls Kashmir exodus trauma, says Muslims helped him
content: Stand-up comedian Samay Raina has once again spoken about the deep scars left by the Kashmiri Pandit exodus, this time offering a more personal and detailed account of how it impacted his family across generations. Recalling the fear that gripped his family, Raina revealed how his maternal grandfather was directly under threat. Amid the violence, Raina highlighted how his family managed to escape.

title: 'They made a big mistake': PM Modi directs ministers to expose opposition's 'anti-women' stand
content: Prime Minister Modi asserted that opposition parties will face repercussions for not backing the women's reservation bill, urging ministers to highlight their "anti-women" stance. He reiterated the government's commitment to implement the reservation by 2029, accusing the opposition of a "big mistake" and attempting to cover up their actions after the bill's defeat in Lok Sabha.

title: Allahabad HC stays FIR order against Rahul Gandhi in dual citizenship case
content: The Allahabad high court has temporarily halted its order for an FIR against Rahul Gandhi concerning alleged dual citizenship. The court emphasized the need to hear the accused before any decision. This follows an earlier directive to register a case based on claims of concealed British citizenship during company incorporation. The probe's transfer to a central agency was also permitted.

title: ‘No one can call me anti-women’: Shashi Tharoor shares lighthearted exchange with Kiren Rijiju
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor shared a lighthearted exchange with Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju, where Rijiju conceded Tharoor couldn't be labelled anti-women. This follows the defeat of a bill proposing 33% women's reservation in legislatures, which aimed to increase Lok Sabha seats. The government's proposal faced opposition, with the Congress criticising the timing and linkage to delimitation.

title: Tim David denies single, falls next ball as costly blunder triggers RCB slide
content: Tim David's refusal of a single proved costly as Royal Challengers Bengaluru faltered in their chase against Delhi Capitals. David's dismissal for 26 off 17 balls, after turning down a run, marked a significant shift in momentum. Earlier, a disciplined bowling effort from Delhi restricted RCB to 175 for 8, despite a strong start from Phil Salt.

title: ‘Troubles DMK, Congress to see ordinary women rise’: PM Modi at Coimbatore rally
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed pain and anger over the failure of the women's reservation bill, accusing the DMK, Congress, and their allies of engaging in "hatred and petty politics." He stated that the DMK "harms women" and "doesn't stand with women in parliament," predicting a response for their "anti-women stand"

title: Gunfire in Hormuz: Indian-flagged tanker among ships targeted as Iran closes strait again
content: Mixed signals from Iran regarding the Strait of Hormuz have caused Indian and Greek crude tankers to turn back amid heightened tensions. Uncertainty over passage through the vital chokepoint deepened after conflicting reports from Tehran, with some merchant ships also reporting gunfire in the troubled waterway.

title: $50,000M revenue blow: What led to half a billion barrels being erased during Iran war
content: A nearly 50-day Iran war has crippled global oil markets, removing over 500 million barrels and costing $50 billion. This unprecedented energy shock, impacting Gulf producers severely, could have long-lasting effects. While the Strait of Hormuz has reopened and a ceasefire is anticipated, a full recovery for energy supplies is expected to be a slow, multi-year process.

title: Women quota bill fails in Lok Sabha: Strategic move or poor planning?
content: For the first time since 2014, the Modi government failed to pass a bill in Parliament, a move analysts suggest was strategic. By forcing a vote on the women's reservation bill, linked to delimitation, the BJP aimed to record every MP's stance, creating a potent campaign tool. The government positioned itself as pro-women reformists, while blaming opposition parties for obstructing progress. This tactic seeks to shape the political narrative ahead of crucial elections, highlighting intent versus obstruction.

title: Kerr on future: These jobs have 'expiration date'
content: Steve Kerr acknowledged his future with the Warriors was uncertain following a season-ending loss to the Suns, saying, "When the run ends, sometimes it's time for new blood and new ideas."

title: Spurs motivated, inspired by Popovich address
content: Hall of Famer and former coach Gregg Popovich, who continues to rehab from a stroke, addressed the Spurs prior to their playoff series against the Trail Blazers.

title: Boone: Cole needs several more rehab outings
content: Yankees ace Gerrit Cole said he had a "solid" rehab outing Friday night, but manager Aaron Boone said the veteran right-hander will need "several more" starts before rejoining the big league club.

title: Spurs concede last-gasp goal to stay in drop zone
content: Tottenham remain in the Premier League relegation zone after they were stunned by a stoppage-time leveller from Brighton substitute Georginio Rutter .

title: Mets put struggling Polanco on IL with sore wrist
content: Mets designated hitter Jorge Polanco has been placed on the 10-day injured list with a right wrist contusion.

title: OKC remains title betting fave; Spurs, C's follow
content: The Thunder (+110) remain large betting favorites to repeat as NBA champions, but the betting public also likes the Spurs (+500) and Celtics (+550) to give Oklahoma City a run for its money.

title: Fever's Boston lands record $6.3M extension
content: Three-time WNBA All-Star Aliyah Boston has agreed to a four-year, $6.3 million contract extension with the Fever, her agent told ESPN.

content: Retired sports stars Sue Bird and Megan Rapinoe announced on social media that, after a decade together, they are no longer in a relationship.

content: Some of the burning issues faced by residents of Gummidipoondi constituency are the expansion of the industrial waste disposal facility in SIPCOT industrial complex and health hazards, discharge of sewage into Thamarai lake, lack of public transport facilities, and poor health infrastructure

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Salem North constituency residents demand shifting of two TASMAC outlets
content: At the Kannankurichi town panchayat, most of the lands have been developed into housing plots in the last 15 years and people are willing to buy lands in this panchayat as it is situated near the foothills of Yercaud.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Coonoor constituency needs a sustainable development model
content: For years, residents have demanded that a sewage treatment plant be set up to treat the town’s waste water, which flows downstream and joins the Bhavani River

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Bhavani constituency faces infrastructure gaps, livelihood concerns
content: The Bhavani jamakkalam sector, despite receiving a GI tag in 2005, is struggling for survival. Weavers say proliferation of cheaper powerloom products has severely affected the market for authentic handwoven items.

title: Residents demand boost for small industries and water supply for agriculture in Jolarpet
content: As per estimates, around 40,000 persons, mostly women, were directly engaged in 550 clusters of incense stick-making units

title: T.N. election 2026: Vaniyambadi constituency residents complain about delay in construction of ROB, illegal sand mining on Palar riverbed
content: They said that indiscriminate sand mining has affected the depth of the riverbed and recharge of groundwater

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Touted as cement hub, but for Ariyalur constituency voters the future appears grey
content: Residents, who blame successive governments for failing to invest adequately and prioritise the district, feel that the constituency would have changed to a great extent if it had high-profile leaders

title: T.N. Assembly election 2026: Pollution by closed chromium factory, untreated effluents remain unsolved in Ranipet constituency
content: Another issue that plagues voters in the constituency is the lack of proper connectivity by buses and trains

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Manamadurai constituency voters seek tangible changes
content: While the constituency encompassed three blocks – Thirupuvanam, Ilayangudi and Manamadurai – none of them had witnessed any industrial or education growth in the past five years, the public felt.

content: Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) describes the meeting as the Ambassador being “called in”, not  “summoned”

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Inadequate infrastructure plagues Madhavaram constituency amid rapid urbanisation
content: Madhavaram constituency struggles with lack of infrastructure, related problems such as solid waste management and piped drinking water supply. Establishing a Tech City and storm-water drains, developing the lakes, and improving the civic infrastructure will remain top priorities, says MLA

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Encroachments, traffic congestion remain core issues in Anna Nagar constituency
content: Unregulated parking is one of the major issues faced by Anna Nagar residents; they say industrial effluents are being discharged into Otteri Nullah waterway, leading to groundwater contamination and demand the shifting of all mobile eateries to non-residential areas in the constituency

title: Siddhartha Academy students bagged 1,936 job offers in 2025-26 academic year, says V-C
content: Students of core branches have bagged the packages ranging between ₹9 LPA to ₹14.5 LPA, says Venkateswara Rao

content: The distribution marked the fourth phase of a special mobile recovery drive conducted at the district police headquarters

title: Opposition to move fresh motion seeking removal of CEC Gyanesh Kumar

content: Abhay S. Oka was delivering a lecture on ‘Environment- Rights or duties under the Constitution of India’ hosted by Rakesh Law Foundation

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Livelihood security, affordable housing are major concerns in Royapuram constituency
content: Residents want the government to implement social welfare measures for the economically disadvantaged. Other demands include redevelopment of dilapidated TNUHDB houses, establishment of a maternity hospital, and flood mitigation measures, particularly in areas along the coastline

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Lack of civic amenities irks Thiruporur constituency residents as urbanisation takes toll
content: The expansion of Chennai’s IT Corridor over the past couple of decades has resulted in the mushrooming of large multi-storeyed apartments, extending well into the villages on either side of the road.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Modakkurichi struggles to bridge gap between agrarian strength and development
content: Modakkurichi entered the national spotlight during the 1996 Assembly election, when 1,016 farmers contested as independents to press for their demands. This highlighted deep agrarian distress and contributed to electoral reforms. Many of the issues raised then, particularly those related to farm incomes, irrigation and infrastructure, persist.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Industry thrives, but pollution, water scarcity haunt Perundurai constituency
content: The constituency sustains over one lakh migrant labourers engaged in the SIPCOT Industrial Estate, the Special Economic Zone complex, textile mills, construction, powerloom units, and the hospitality sector. However, residents say the benefits of industrialisation have come at a cost, as they continue to face environmental degradation.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Once a Chief Minister’s citadel, Srirangam constituency yearns for more infrastructure development
content: With a major temple town in its midst and rapid urbanisation of its rural segments, the civic infrastructure in the constituency has been stretched to its limits in recent years

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Katpadi constituency residents seek solution to heavy traffic congestion, lack of jobs
content: Since 1996, the DMK’s strongman Duraimurugan, party’s general secretary and Minister for Water Resources now, has won six consecutive times, making it his own fort. In 2021, however, Mr. Duraimurugan won with a margin of a mere 746 votes.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Tambaram constituency residents feel rapid growth continues to be marred by basic infrastructure issues
content: The Tambaram constituency lacks the civic amenities to tackle rapid urbanisation as arterial roads often face traffic congestion; untreated sewage is being discharged into lakes, and the interior roads remain in poor shape; MLA says drainage system has been implemented in the core areas

title: Darjeeling hills to witness multi-pronged contest on diverse issues in 2026 Assembly polls
content: While the BJP-GJM alliance and the Trinamool-BGPM alliance are clashing head-on, a new hill party by local entrepreneur Ajay Edwards is also making its presence; along with the pertinent issue of Gorkhaland political situation, the issues of development and corruption may sway the electorate this election season

content: Telangana Chief Minister Revanth Reddy says BJP using women’s reservation as a cover to change the Constitution and end reservation for Dalits and tribals

content: A majority of the people in the constituency are daily wagers, farmers, and those involved in silver anklet manufacturing at Selathampatti, Sivathapuram, Panankadu, and Sooramangalam.

title: HC grants bail to 2 former Congress leaders arrested in Leh violence case
content: a large crowd gathered outside the district jail in leh on saturday to welcome the release of former Congress councillor Smanla Dorje Nurboo (36) and former Congress J&K MLA Deldan Namgail (47) after the high court granted them bail . police had accused the two of instigating the violence and being present at the scene .

title: 10 NFL scouts predict the first 10 draft picks
content: scouts are unaffiliated with the teams they were assigned . none even work for a franchise in the same division as the teams . the 2026 draft is set to begin April 23 in Pittsburgh . a team from the other cnn team will be assigned to each pick .

title: Wyshynski predicts the entire 2026 Stanley Cup playoff bracket
content: the 2026 Stanley Cup playoffs are here, and they're weird . the Buffalo Sabres , who last made the playoffs when "Fast Five" was in theaters, are here . but that's why the tournament is the greatest postseason in sports: the unpredictability .

title: Who's winning the Stanley Cup? All the intel you need on the 16 playoff teams
content: the Eastern Conference bracket was set earlier this week, including the Carolina Hurricanes and Buffalo Sabres as division champions . the Edmonton Oilers and Anaheim Ducks will face off as the Nos. 2 and 3 seeds, while the Los Angeles Kings will play the Avalanche as the second wild card .
